message:
fix(fr-command): XGROUP CREATE accepts ENTRIESREAD form, uses subcommand-syntax-error wording [bd-frankenredis-wpch]

Upstream xgroupCommand parses XGROUP CREATE as:
  XGROUP CREATE key groupname id|$ [MKSTREAM] [ENTRIESREAD entries-read]
MKSTREAM and ENTRIESREAD are independent option tokens that may
appear in either order. For invalid shapes (argc < 5 or unrecognised
options token), upstream calls addReplySubcommandSyntaxError
producing 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for
CREATE. Try XGROUP HELP.'. ENTRIESREAD value parses via
getLongLongFromObjectOrReply.

fr-command strictly required argc ∈ {5, 6} so any ENTRIESREAD form
was wrong-arity rejected, and emitted 'syntax error' /
'wrong number of arguments for xgroup|create command' instead of
the addReplySubcommandSyntaxError wording.

Replace the rigid 5/6-argc gate with a token loop that accepts
MKSTREAM and ENTRIESREAD <int> in any order, validating ENTRIESREAD
via parse_i64_arg (raw 'value is not an integer or out of range'
on bad input). The parsed entries-read value is currently ignored
since fr's xgroup model doesn't track it yet. Update the
xgroup_validation test to pin the new arity wording.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches all six
divergent cases after fix.
